subject: Mathematics
title: Shapes and Angles

sections.heading: Shapes and Angles
sections.content:
Rohini made this shape.
Mohini — Is it a closed shape or an open shape?
Rohini It is a closed shape.
Mohini How many sides are there?
Rohini It has 6 sides.
Mohini made this.
Now you give the answers.
—
—
—
Oh! That is
so simple.
2 Shapes and Angles
Rohini and Mohini are twin sisters. They love doing the same
things. One day when they were making shapes with matchsticks,
Shaila gave them a challenge. Rohini will make a shape.
Mohini has to make the
same without looking at it,
but she can ask questions.
Is it a closed shape? Does it have
6 sides? .
same as the one made by
Rohini So Mohini tried again.
This is what she made.
_______.
But it is not the
.
________

3) Four different angles are marked in four colours. Can you find
other angles which are the same as the one marked in red?
Mark them in red. Do this for the other colours.
Practice Time
1) Look at the shape and answer.
The angle marked in ________ colour is the biggest angle.h
2 (a) Are the angles marked with yellow equal? ________
b) Are the angles marked with green equal? ________
c) Are the angles marked with blue equal? ________

In my name there are 11 right angles. There are also 10 angles
less than a right angle.
h Write 3 names using straight lines and count the angles.
You know,
there are
angles in the
letters of our
names too.
Number of
right angles
Number of angles
more than a right angle
Name Number of angles
less than a right angle
Activity
a) Put 10 Math-Magic books on top of each
other. Keep one book slanting to make a slide.
b) Now do this with six books.
h R From which slide does the ball rolloll a ball from the top.
down faster?
h Which slide has the smaller angle?
h Look for the birds which have beaks with angles smaller than a
right angle.
h In the picture mark angles between the two branches. Which
two branches have the biggest angle?
Angles in Names

learningObjectives: Understand the key concepts of Shapes and Angles
Identify and explain the main ideas covered in this chapter
Apply knowledge from this chapter to real-world situations
Recall important terms and definitions from Shapes and Angles
